_note: Industry feed: Space / EO / climate / maritime news in dry insider voice. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Polish startup Eycore launches its first SAR satellite, becoming Europe's second privately-owned SAR operator
items.snark: Second privately-owned SAR operator in Europe is a milestone until you remember ICEYE has been at it since 2018 and already has the customer contracts.

items.headline: EarthDaily Analytics puts six satellites into orbit, announces NRO contract and eight-figure defense subscription
items.snark: Daily global EO at scale with a defense subscription sounds compelling, but now comes the hard part of proving the constellation economics actually work.

items.headline: Planet Labs achieves onboard AI object detection on Pelican-4 satellite, identifying aircraft in real time
items.snark: Onboard AI finally working in orbit after years of talk, though the jump from identifying parked planes to actionable maritime intelligence remains steep.

items.headline: Air Force Secretary says US needs new launch site as military space launch demand approaches 1,000 flights by 2031
items.snark: Cape and Vandenberg are tapped out at current trajectories, but finding a politically viable third launch complex will take longer than building the infrastructure.

items.headline: Loft Orbital wins French space agency contract to build 10-satellite multi-sensor EO constellation
items.snark: Multi-sensor architecture with onboard processing for CNES is ambitious, assuming France's space budgets hold steady through the full deployment timeline.

items.headline: Satellogic announces Merlin constellation for daily one-meter global remapping, first launch October 2026
items.snark: One-meter daily global coverage sounds transformative until the subscription pricing appears and customers realize monitoring everything is harder than monitoring something.

items.headline: Global Fishing Watch maps 100% of industrial fishing fleet using Sentinel-2 optical data, tripling detection capacity
items.snark: Mapping the entire industrial fleet is a technical win, but enforcement still depends on coastal states with variable political will and patrol budgets.

items.headline: Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation shows consistent weakening across 20-year monitoring period
items.snark: AMOC decline finally captured with direct observations, giving modelers firmer ground and policymakers another data point they will probably ignore.

items.headline: Southern Ocean showing intensified precipitation at Macquarie Island, suggesting ocean may be 'sweating' faster under warming
items.snark: One remote island rain gauge is signaling systemic change in the Southern Ocean, which is either a canary or an outlier depending on the next five years of data.

items.headline: Filipino Ocean Hero Norlan Pagal dies at 56 after decade defending Tañon Strait from wheelchair following 2015 shooting
items.snark: Pagal spent ten years taking dynamite and bullets for marine enforcement in waters the government struggled to patrol, a reminder that conservation has a human cost.

items.headline: Mongabay investigation shows enforcement gaps persist in global marine protected areas despite expansion pledges
items.snark: Creating MPAs is politically easy, staffing patrol vessels and prosecuting violators is not, which is why satellite monitoring keeps gaining budget share.

items.headline: NOAA issues El Niño Watch with 82% probability of emergence by July 2026, expected to continue into 2027
items.snark: Another El Niño cycle will stress fisheries from Peru to Indonesia, giving illegal operators more cover as enforcement resources shift to disaster response.
